title: Zero to One: Notes on Startups, or How to Build the Future
description: A contrarian guide to startup success that argues monopolies — not competition — drive innovation, and that true progress means creating something new rather than copying what already works.
keyConcepts: Seven questions every business must answer
notableQuotes: What important truth do very few people agree with you on?
All happy companies are different: each one earns a monopoly by solving a unique problem. All failed companies are the same: they failed to escape competition.
We were promised flying cars, and we got 140 characters.
The most contrarian thing of all is not to oppose the crowd but to think for yourself.
Brilliant thinking is rare, but courage is in even shorter supply than genius.
